reasoning: kaa with an adjective (ñíɁní ‘hot’) after the noun Nduča ‘water’ marks ‘The water is hot’ with subject focus.
kúu selects a noun complement; ɨɨ xasúčí is ‘a young person’ (xa- nominalizes súčí ‘young’).
Predicate–subject order (ÑíɁní nduča) gives predicate focus: ‘The water is hot.’
kaa + adjective lúlí gives ‘Juan is small/short.’
Ndežu ‘food’ + kaa + žaɁu ‘expensive’ → ‘The food is expensive,’ with subject focus.
Adjective-first Súčí Maria expresses ‘Maria is young.’
kúu + noun xažiirí ‘my husband’ → ‘Juan is my husband.’
kúu + noun xalúlírí ‘my child’ → ‘Pedro is my child.’
Kaa + adjective kwíí ‘green’ with no overt subject means ‘It is green.’
xa- nominalizes adjectives to nouns: kǔnú ‘deep’ → xakǔnú ‘depth’.
xa- nominalizes ñíɁní ‘hot’ → xañíɁní ‘heat’.
kaa links subjects with adjectives; ndeɁé ‘brave’.
kaa with adjective súkú ‘tall’.
kúu takes a noun phrase; xa- nominalizes súkú ‘tall’ and ɨɨ is the indefinite ‘a’.
Ndežu ‘fruit/food’ used as the noun; kwaɁá ‘red’; kaa links noun with adjective.
-rí marks 1sg possession (‘my fruit’); kúu with nominalized adjective xakwíí ‘the green one’.
With no overt subject, kaa + adjective ndáa ‘true’ means ‘It is true.’
Predicate-first order gives an alternative focused ‘It is true.’
Truth is a noun formed by xa- nominalization; noun complements use kúu.

answer: Two copulas: kaa links subjects with adjectives; kúu links subjects with nouns. Word order marks focus: Subject–kaa–ADJ focuses the subject; ADJ–Subject focuses the predicate; kaa–ADJ with no subject means ‘It is ADJ.’ The prefix xa- nominalizes adjectives into nouns meaning ‘one/thing that is ADJ’ (e.g., súčí → xasúčí ‘young person’, ndáa → xandáa ‘truth’). ɨɨ is an indefinite article ‘a’. The suffix -rí marks ‘my’ and can attach to nouns or nominalized forms (e.g., xalúlírí ‘my child’).
reasoning: Summarizes the morphosyntax inferred from the pairings and translations.
